name: Feature adoption tips
description: Guide users after they adopt a feature by sending a short series of tips that help them reach key activation milestones. We're using PostHog Workflows as an example in this template.

actions.name: User starts using a feature
actions.description: Select the event that shows the user has started using the feature.

actions.name: Tip 1
actions.description: Send the first tip to the user.

value.html:
Congrats on your first workflow

Congrats – you created your first workflow. The hedgehogs are proud.

Most teams don’t stop at one. A few simple ones can save a surprising amount of manual work. Things like:

 

• sending alerts when something breaks
• nudging users who get stuck
• celebrating users who do something great

 

Workflows are basically tiny robots that do annoying tasks for you. Might be worth building one more.
Build another workflow
 

p.s. This email was sent with PostHog Workflows! 🦔

Boring, legal bit: We're at 2261 Market Street #4008, CA 94114. Unsubscribe?

actions.name: Milestone activated?
actions.description: Check if the user has activated the key milestone.

actions.name: Tip 2
actions.description: Send a second tip to the user.

value.html:
What to expect when self-hosting PostHog

Quick tip: workflows get a lot more useful once you add a bit of logic. Instead of doing the same thing every time, you can branch based on what users actually do.

 

For example:

 

• if a user signs up → send welcome message
• if a payment fails → alert the team
• if someone leaves negative feedback → investigate immediately

 

Basically: fewer generic automations, more useful ones.
Give it a try
 

p.s. This email was sent with PostHog Workflows! 🦔

Boring, legal bit: We're at 2261 Market Street #4008, CA 94114. Unsubscribe?

actions.name: Milestone activated?
actions.description: Check if the user has activated the key milestone.

actions.name: Tip 3
actions.description: Send a third tip to the user

value.html:
Did you flip the “on” switch?

One last tip. If you haven’t already, make sure your workflow is enabled. Otherwise it’s just sitting there looking nice.

 

Once it’s turned on, it can automatically send messages, notify your team, or trigger actions based on real user behavior.

 

Set it loose and let it do the boring work.
Let's automate

Or, check our self-hosting docs
 

p.s. This email was sent with PostHog Workflows! 🦔
